steps.title: Clear the fractions
steps.content: Start with the equation $\frac{1}{6xy}+xyz=\frac{1}{5yz}$. Since $x$, $y$, and $z$ are positive, multiply every term by $30xyz$. This common multiple clears both fractions without changing the solution set.

steps.title: Isolate the variable term
steps.content:
After multiplying, the three terms become $5z$, $30x^2y^2z^2$, and $6x$. Collect the terms with $y^2$ and divide by $30x^2z^2$.
$5z+30x^2y^2z^2=6x$
$30x^2z^2y^2=6x-5z$
$y^2=\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}$

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content:
Because $y$ is positive, use the positive square root.
$y=\sqrt{\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}}$
The answer is B.
